predictions.reasoning: Diary is unambiguously positive: explicit 'feel great', engagement in favorite activity (garden coffee), weather appreciation. Behavioral data fully corroborates: outdoor environment confirmed, walking anomalously high (Z=+8.06 vs typical 2.4 min morning baseline) consistent with garden time, social app use (1h 5m) and 42 music tracks reinforce positive mood. Profile shows high trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 on average, but personal history documents garden/outdoor activities as a reliable positive affect source. No NA indicators in any channel. ER desire near floor.

Diary presents a clear stress-but-resilient profile: explicit high work stress (NA elevated, worried) but strong positive reframe ('still feeling good overall') indicating maintained PA. Behavioral data strongly corroborates: massively elevated walking (z=+63.80, 34 min vs ~0.6 min typical) in 8 episodes suggests repeated outdoor stress-coping breaks — consistent with this person's known pattern of outdoor activity as a coping mechanism. Music listening (42 tracks) and social app dominance further signal active mood regulation. Morning EMA confirmed very positive start (weather, garden); afternoon stress has built but not derailed overall state. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 suggest baseline negativity tendency, but today they are functioning above their usual level. ER_desire elevated slightly from morning's 0 due to acknowledged work stress, making ER_desire_State=true. Available (outdoor, active, not isolated).

Diary is unambiguously positive: quality family time with husband and daughter plus intentional self-reward (favorite ice cream) — the two primary coping/joy anchors in this person's history. Behavioral data strongly corroborates: walking 34 min (Z=+44, top 1% for their evening baseline) and outdoor environment align with an ice cream outing; social apps dominate screen use (1h 5m of 1h 43m total); music (42 tracks) reinforces positive mood. Both screen use and walking are dramatically above personal evening baseline, marking this as a distinctly positive, out-of-routine evening. Day arc resolved well: morning positivity → afternoon work stress (15:05 EMA) → warm family landing. Despite high trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19, this moment is clearly grounded in positive affect with minimal regulation desire. INT_availability=no because active family engagement with young child leaves little bandwidth for intervention.

Diary explicitly reports poor sleep — the dominant signal for this morning EMA. For P458 (PHQ-8=19, GAD-7=12, trait NA=31), sleep disruption reliably elevates NA and suppresses PA. Behavioral data corroborates: elevated screen use and heavy social media (3h 5m) are consistent with fatigue-driven distraction-seeking; elevated walking z-score may reflect restlessness. Overnight screen data is absent (device off), so cannot confirm nocturnal awakenings, but diary is unambiguous. Yesterday was broadly positive (garden morning, family evening), making today's sleep disruption a clear negative departure. ER_desire estimated at 2 — slightly elevated vs yesterday's 0 pattern, reflecting mild distress but not acute need. INT_availability predicted no: poor sleep state reduces openness to interventions despite morning availability pattern.

Brief work-focus diary is emotionally neutral — no distress, no expressed joy, consistent with functional engagement. Behavioral data (sedentary indoors, keyboard activity, background music) strongly corroborates work-focused state. High social app usage (3h 5m) may reflect work messaging or social breaks. Walking above baseline (z=+9.2) suggests at least one activity break. Clinical backdrop (PHQ-8=19, GAD-7=12, Trait NA=31) anchors moderate chronic negative affect and worried state despite neutral diary. Morning EMA showed ER_desire=3 after poor sleep; afternoon work focus likely provides mild buffering, bringing ER_desire slightly lower (~2). PANAS_Pos moderate (13) reflecting task engagement but low trait PA; PANAS_Neg elevated (10) consistent with high trait NA and clinical depression. INT_availability=yes as afternoon work state is functional and participant shows prior pattern of availability during afternoon EMAs.

Diary describes a joyful family evening picking out garden plants with daughter — gardening is a documented positive coping activity for this participant. Walking 39 min vs. typical 2.8 min baseline (z=+6.28) validates active outdoor/nursery trip. Most behaviorally similar day (0.96, 04-29 evening) showed ER_desire=0, INT_availability=yes with identical family-time context. Day trajectory improved from rough morning (poor sleep, ER=3) to positive evening. Despite high trait NA (31) and PHQ-8 (19), family+gardening reliably produce low-NA, high-PA states for this person. No stressors in diary; appreciative 'got to' framing signals gratitude.

Diary 'I slept well last night' directly contrasts yesterday's bad-sleep morning (ER_desire=3), predicting low ER desire. Behavioral evidence is consistent: outdoor environment, music engagement (49 tracks), moderate social app use — matching prior positive-state mornings (4/29 garden+coffee pattern). Device offline overnight confirms normal sleep. Despite high trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 setting a chronic depressive floor, no acute stressors appear in diary or sensing. Morning EMAs for this individual consistently show INT_availability=yes. PANAS_Neg kept moderate (not low) due to stable trait-level depression/anxiety.

Diary 'Being focused on work' is nearly identical to 4/30 15:14 (ER=0, available=yes). Behavioral data strongly aligns: high keyboard activity (3524 chars), elevated screen time, 49 music tracks — all consistent with focused work engagement. Good sleep this morning rules out fatigue-driven stress (the only prior ER>0 case). Baseline comparisons show elevated but contextually expected activity. All 7 prior EMAs show receptive=no; top-2 similar days both ER_desire=0. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 suggest background vulnerability, but no acute distress signal in diary or behavior. PA_State=true (work engagement + good sleep = above personal norm). INT_availability=yes follows the dominant pattern (6/7 entries).

Diary explicitly states recurrence fear ('fearing a recurrence a bit this evening') and a heavy emotional discussion with husband about a second child, anchored to the trauma of being diagnosed when their first child was 8 months old. Fear and worry are confirmed; 'a bit' moderates severity. Behavioral data shows screen use at Z=+11.51 (massively anomalous vs. evening baseline), heavy social app use (52min), no entertainment, and 49 music tracks — consistent with anxious preoccupation and active emotion regulation rather than relaxation. Despite behavioral similarity to positive family evenings (04-29/04-30, sim=0.93), diary content diverges sharply: this is emotionally charged, not routine family time. High trait NA=31, PHQ-8=19, GAD-7=12 amplify the signal. Husband presence mitigates loneliness and moderates severity — interaction quality is high. Forecasting is active (second child deliberation). ER_desire elevated from today's earlier baseline (0-1) but moderate given husband support. INT_availability predicted yes per established evening pattern, but historical receptivity is consistently no.

Diary signals unambiguous positive affect: deliberate choice to work outside on patio, appreciating beautiful weather, enthusiastic tone. Behavioral data perfectly corroborates — outdoor environment detected, music listening (24 tracks), elevated walking (48 min vs ~1 min/hr baseline, z=+19), social app engagement. No stress indicators. Historical pattern: 8/9 prior EMAs show INT_availability=yes; similar behaviorally-matched days all show ER_desire=0 with available=yes. Trait burden (PHQ-8=19, GAD-7=12) acknowledged but state today is clearly above personal positive baseline, consistent with their established pattern of nature-linked mood uplift.

Diary signals mild work unmotivation offset by positive self-appraisal ('overall feeling good'). Behavioral data supports: heavy social app use (2h 3m) and 465 screen opens consistent with distraction during low-motivation work; outdoor environment aligns with morning patio decision; light keyboard output (1650 chars) corroborates low work engagement. Three most similar past days all show ER_desire=0, INT=yes — this behavioral signature is low-distress. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8 (19) contribute moderate background negative affect even on positive days. No fear/pain/loneliness signals present. INT_availability consistently yes in afternoon entries.

Diary is unambiguously positive: proactive self-care (hired cleaner) followed by quality garden time with daughter — this person's two most reliable emotional anchors per their baseline profile. Walking (48 min vs baseline 2.2 min, Z=+10) and outdoor environment tag physically confirm the garden activity. The 3 most behaviorally similar past days all show ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=yes, with near-identical evening family/garden diary entries. Afternoon ER_desire=3 (work unmotivation) has been fully resolved by the restorative evening activity. High trait NA (31) tempers NA to near-floor but not zero; PANAS_Neg reflects residual background anxiety rather than any active stressor. No recurrence fears, no financial stress, no fatigue signals in this entry.

Diary is maximally vague ('not sure it has been anything specific') — no positive or negative anchors identified. Morning EMA after a positive prior evening (house cleaner, quality daughter time). High trait NA/PHQ-8=19 sets a background floor for PANAS_Neg, but no acute distress markers. All 3 most similar behavioral days show ER_desire=0, available=yes. Historical INT_availability pattern is 11/12 yes. Outdoor activity + music suggests mild engagement, not withdrawal. Flat affect is the dominant interpretation: neither state dimension clearly elevated above personal baseline.

Diary describes evening family time with husband and daughter — identical to his 3 most behaviorally similar past days (sim=0.95–1.00), all of which produced ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=yes. Behavioral data fully corroborates: social apps dominated (2h17m), outdoor environment, elevated walking (+10 SD), music — active engaged family day. No stressors mentioned. Despite high trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19, this person reliably shows low NA and positive affect during family evenings. Pattern is extremely consistent across the session history.

Diary signals excellent morning wellbeing: good sleep and pleasant weather, both confirmed by outdoor environment in behavioral data. 31 music tracks and moderate activity align with upbeat state. All 3 most-similar past days show ER_desire=0 and available=yes. Prior mornings with sleep quality or weather praise uniformly had ER_desire=0. Despite high Trait NA/PHQ-8, state-level affect on good mornings is consistently positive for this participant. No stressors, health fears, or work mentions suppress NA estimates.

Diary 'Not sure' is maximally ambiguous — no emotional content. Behavioral evidence is relatively positive: outdoor environment, 31 music tracks, elevated walking (32m vs 2.6m baseline, Z=+6.53). Three most similar past days (similarity 0.98-0.99) all showed ER_desire=0/INT_availability=yes with positive family-time diaries. Morning EMA was explicitly positive. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 set a moderate background NA floor, but no behavioral or diary signal indicates an elevated negative episode today. Predicting neutral-flat state with trait-level mild NA, low ER desire, and availability consistent with past similar evenings.

Diary 'Good sleep' is a minimal but unambiguous positive signal. All 3 prior morning EMAs with good-sleep diaries (05-01, 05-04, 04-29) resulted in ER_desire=0, INT_availability=yes. The 4 most behaviorally similar days all show the same receptivity pattern (0/4 receptive). Weekend behavioral profile — high social app usage, moderate activity, indoor/relaxed — aligns with calm positive mood. Despite high trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19, this person's mornings after good sleep are consistently low-NA. No stressors, cancer fears, or work-related language present.

Diary centers on financial stress from cancer treatment debt — chronic, specific stressor causing evening rumination. Behavioral signature aligns: screen time massively elevated (Z=+13, heavy social browsing), music playing (mood regulation), above-baseline walking (restlessness). Most similar past days had ER_desire=0 with positive diaries; the closest emotional analog (recurrence fear, 05-01) had ER_desire=3. Today's stressor is real but language is reflective rather than acutely distressed, placing ER_desire at 2. High trait NA (31), PHQ-8=19 background context. Worried/afraid/sad states likely given financial-cancer linkage; INT_availability consistently yes throughout session history even under stress.

Diary 'Being focused on work' matches two historical anchors (2024-04-30, 2024-05-01) that both yielded ER_desire=0-1 and INT_availability=yes. Morning EMA; this person shows consistent morning resets even after high-NA evenings (yesterday ER_desire=7 financial stress). Behavioral narrative supports work mode: indoor, stationary, music (24 tracks). Social app usage (57 min) is slightly high for stated work focus, suggesting mild passive distraction but not acute distress. PHQ-8=19/GAD-7=12 set a moderate NA floor, but diary and historical pattern anchor toward calm functional state. Device off pre-EMA so no acute morning sensor signal. High confidence driven by near-identical prior diary entries.

Diary 'Stress at work' is a terse, unambiguous negative signal — the absence of any positive qualifier (unlike 04-29 'but still feeling good overall') indicates purer stress. Behavioral pattern corroborates: elevated social media use (57min, ~40% of screen time) and music (24 tracks) suggest escapist/coping phone behavior; 49min walking vs personal baseline of 2.1min (z=+12.45) may reflect restless pacing. Low keyboard activity (840 chars) consistent with diminished productivity under stress. Morning ER_desire=1; afternoon work stress bump pushes ER_desire to ~3. High trait NA (31) and GAD-7=12 amplify work stress impact. INT_availability: 18/19 prior EMAs were 'yes' including prior work-stress episodes — strong base rate for yes.

Diary signals mixed state: family time provides emotional uplift (pleased, grateful, interactions quality) but 'still very tired' indicates persistent physical/emotional fatigue elevating NA. Three behaviorally similar days all show ER_desire=0 with family-time evening diaries, strongly predicting low intervention desire. Midday work stress (ER=7) likely dissipated with family transition. High trait NA (31) and clinical depression (PHQ-8=19) keep negative states elevated above personal baseline despite positive family context. Passive behavioral profile (stationary, social media, music) confirms low-energy but content home evening.

Diary directly reports a mixed state: explicit happiness alongside 'very stressed' about work-life balance and mental health management with parenting demands. Behavioral data corroborates stress: today's screen use (405min) is significantly elevated vs the prior 3 days (127-242min), and heavy social media engagement (2h41m) is consistent with stress-driven distraction. Music listening (27 tracks) aligns with this participant's coping pattern. Back-to-back high ER_desire days (May 5-6, both ER=7) for financial and work stress suggest accumulating burden. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 elevate baseline negative affect. Morning EMA context (pre-work) means positive mood is present but stress is anticipated. INT_availability predicted 'yes' based on near-universal 'yes' pattern across all prior sessions regardless of state.

Diary 'I'm not sure' is maximally ambiguous, mirroring prior vague entries (05-04 ER=0; 05-03 ER=0). Critical context: this morning's EMA (08:51) documented explicit work stress + mental health balancing concerns with ER=3, providing background elevated NA. Screen usage is an extreme outlier — z=+37.53 above personal baseline — suggesting avoidance/distraction behavior consistent with ongoing anxiety or low mood. Social app dominance (2h41m), high music consumption (27 tracks), and sedentary indoor posture form a passive-consumption pattern typical of stress-coping. PHQ=19 (moderately severe depression), GAD=12 (moderate anxiety), and Trait NA=31 anchor the baseline high. Most similar behavioral day (05-02) had ER=0 (positive family day), but 05-05 (behaviorally equivalent) had ER=7 due to financial stress — suggesting ER is driven by diary content not behavior alone. Given ambiguous diary but confirmed morning stress trajectory, ER=3 (matching morning value) is the best estimate. INT_availability=yes is invariant across all 22 prior session EMAs.

Diary is minimally informative ('End of the work day') — neutral and flat, with no emotional valence either way. Today's earlier EMAs show moderate work stress in the morning (ER_desire=3, diary explicitly mentions work stress + mental health balancing), settling to neutral by afternoon (ER_desire=0). Evening behavioral signature is consistent with winding-down: heavy social/entertainment app use, music (27 tracks), phone set down for 3h before EMA. The work stress thread that ran through the morning likely keeps mild worry elevated, but the absence of any explicit negative signal in the evening diary and the leisure-oriented behavior suggest affect has settled. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8 (19) set a high floor for NA, but individual-level state is estimated below personal baseline given no acute stressor in diary. INT_availability predicted yes based on strong historical pattern (all prior evenings = yes) and home context.

Diary clearly signals positive morning state: good sleep (physiological restoration) plus anticipatory affect from upcoming trip planning. This matches a well-established pattern in session memory — good-sleep morning diaries on 05-04 and 05-05 both showed ER=0. Heavy social app usage (2h22m) is consistent with trip coordination. Music listening (75 tracks) supports upbeat mood. No stress, fear, or negative themes in diary. Similar days predominantly show ER=0/available=yes. Despite high trait NA and PHQ-8=19, this participant demonstrates clear situational positive affect on restful, anticipatory mornings. Trip forecasting adds elevated PA relative to baseline.

Diary 'Work stress.' is nearly identical to 2024-05-06 13:43 'Stress at work.' which produced ER_desire=7 and receptive=YES — the strongest analog in session memory. Work stress is a major recurring stressor for this high-NA (31), moderately-severe-depression (PHQ-8=19) individual. Behavioral data corroborates: very elevated screen use (z=+24.60) dominated by social apps (2h 22m) signals avoidance/distraction from work demands; 75 music tracks indicates active coping. Elevated walking (z=+8.02) consistent with stress-related restlessness. Morning entry was positive (good sleep, upcoming trip), providing slight buffer vs 05-06 peak, hence ER=6 rather than 7. INT_availability predicted yes based on near-universal base rate (24/25 entries). Forecasting=true given morning mention of upcoming trip still likely influencing forward thinking.

Diary 'Not sure!' is an ambivalent, minimal response — same phrasing as prior ER=0 evenings (2024-05-04, 2024-05-03). Morning was positive (good sleep, trip plans); afternoon had moderate work stress (ER=4), but this person reliably winds down by evening. Behavioral narrative shows heavy driving (3h, likely pre-trip errands aligned with morning trip mention) and elevated screen/social use, but no active communication — passive, distracted mode. Similar days split between ER=7 (financial distress) and ER=0 (family wind-down); diary content aligns with the neutral ER=0 cluster. High trait NA/PHQ-8 noted but actual EMA ER desire consistently low. Predicting mild residual stress from work day (ER=2) but no actionable emotional regulation need. INT_availability=yes consistent with all evening readings.

Diary signals a calm, content morning: waking early is framed as a positive opportunity to ease into the day — no stress, anxiety, or negative themes present. Behavioral pattern (outdoor, stationary, brief walks, social app browsing) aligns with their established garden-morning ritual. Historical analog mornings with positive diary tone consistently show ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=yes. Despite high trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19, this is a pre-work morning window before daily stressors emerge. Similar-days lookup confirms all analogues report INT_availability=yes. Predicting moderate PA, suppressed NA, zero ER desire.

Diary 'Not sure!' is ambiguous but historically maps to ER_desire 0-1 and INT_availability=yes for this participant (same text yesterday: ER=1, yes). Morning EMA was positive (easing into the day). Behavioral profile shows outdoor environment, 54 min walking (elevated vs baseline), elevated social app use — consistent with a mild positive/neutral afternoon. No acute stressors (financial stress, work anxiety) mentioned. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8 (19) depress PA estimates, but no behavioral or textual signals of active distress. Predicted as mildly positive and receptive.

Diary 'Being tired' is a pure fatigue signal with no secondary stressors. Session arc went positive morning → uncertain afternoon → fatigued evening. Behavioral data confirms sedentary day (21h stationary) with passive phone consumption pattern (430 brief screen opens, social media dominant). Most analogous historical day is 05/06 evening ('I am still just very tired') which yielded ER_desire=0. Similar-day ER_desire average is 3.7 but driven by days with explicit stressors (recurrence fear, financial burden) absent here. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 anchor NA_State as elevated relative to normative baselines, but this is a typical tired evening for this person rather than an acutely distressed one. INT_availability=yes is near-certain (28/29 historical rate). Low ER_desire predicted given simple fatigue without identified emotional regulation need.

Diary is unambiguously positive: 'I'm happy it is Friday!' — explicit Friday-effect joy with no stress signals. Behavioral evidence is fully consistent: high social app usage (2h 38m), very heavy keyboard activity (53k chars), elevated walking (52 min, Z=+21 above baseline), and active music listening (32 tracks) all indicate engagement and positive affect. Morning device was off (7-10 AM) — normal relaxed morning routine. Historical pattern shows morning EMAs for this user almost universally have ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=yes. Despite high Trait NA (31), no negative activation signals present. Forecasting=true reflecting likely weekend anticipation.

Friday evening with family + explicit weekend anticipation in diary yields strong positive state. Morning diary ('happy it is Friday') reinforces all-day positive valence. Social app dominance (2h 38m) and heavy keyboard activity behaviorally confirm family engagement. No stress, fear, fatigue, or health concerns mentioned. Trait NA=31 and PHQ-8=19 are high baseline, but historical pattern shows this person reliably achieves above-average PA during family time. Similar days (05-01, 05-09, 05-08) all show ER=0-3, available=yes, receptive=no. ER_desire predicted minimal; INT_availability yes matches 30/31 prior entries.

Diary 'The weather and spending time with my mom!' is unambiguously positive — two explicit joy sources (good weather, maternal companionship) on a Saturday with zero stressors mentioned. Behavioral data strongly corroborates: outdoor environment, walking z=+22 (extremely elevated vs. 2.1 min baseline, confirming active outdoor outing), 2h29m driving (traveling to/from mom), social+comm apps 59min. Music enjoyment adds further leisure signal. High trait NA (31) is acknowledged but today shows no activating negative content. Session history shows ER_desire=0 on nearly all positive family-time days with INT_availability=yes consistently.

Diary 'Enjoying time with family' is unambiguous positive affect. Continuous with earlier EMA ('weather + spending time with mom'). Behavioral evidence is fully consistent: outdoor environment, highly elevated walking (Z=+13.77), driving suggesting family outings, social app usage (49m), music (20 tracks). 4/5 similar days show ER_desire=0. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 introduce mild background NA floor, but today's state is clearly above personal positive baseline. No stress, health anxiety, or fatigue signals in either diary or sensing.

Morning Sunday EMA. Diary explicitly positive: 'wonderful day yesterday' after a Saturday spent with mom and family (confirmed by May 11 entries). No stressors mentioned. Weekend pattern for this person consistently shows low NA, low ER_desire, and positive social engagement. Behavioral data corroborates: social apps (1h 8m), music (37 tracks), moderate activity — consistent with a relaxed family weekend. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 calibrate predictions toward personal norms; 'wonderful' represents clear above-baseline positivity. INT_availability=yes as consistent throughout study, but ER_desire=0 means not actually receptive.

Diary explicitly states fatigue is negatively impacting mood — the primary and unambiguous signal. Behavioral data is cross-modally consistent: high stationarity (16h+), passive screen use (351 opens), social media scrolling (1h 8m), and music listening (37 tracks, likely mood regulation) all align with a tired, low-energy afternoon. The shift from the morning's positive entry ('wonderful day yesterday') confirms afternoon fatigue onset. Most similar past days ('Being tired' on 05/09) showed ER_desire=0 with availability=yes, but today's explicit mood-impact framing warrants slightly higher ER_desire (3). High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 elevate baseline negative affect. No acute stressors (work, recurrence, financial) present today. This person has virtually never reported INT_availability=no — one exception in the entire session history.

Diary 'Being tired.' is a minimal fatigue signal — identical phrasing to 2024-05-09 evening (ER_desire=0, most similar day similarity=1.00). Earlier afternoon EMA today also noted tiredness affecting mood (ER_desire=1). Behavioral narrative strongly corroborates: 16h18m stationary, heavy passive screen use (3h13m with social media), music (37 tracks), fully indoor — classic tired-rest pattern. No acute stressor, work stress, cancer concern, or emotional distress mentioned. Weekend Sunday context following family time provides mild positive buffer but energy is depleted by evening. High trait NA (31) and depression history (PHQ-8=19) raise baseline NA, but without explicit distress content, NA state flags remain below threshold. ER_desire estimated at 1 (consistent with tired days, slightly above May 9's 0 given mood impact noted earlier). Interactions_quality flagged true given earlier family time (morning/afternoon with family context). Availability virtually certain given 32/33 prior 'yes' responses.

Diary explicitly attributes current state to menstrual hormonal changes — a recurring theme for this person per baseline history. This signals elevated NA, possible physical pain/discomfort, and mood fluctuations above their already-high trait NA baseline (31). Behavioral data (91 min walking, outdoor, 483 screen opens) shows continued functional engagement, consistent with the matter-of-fact explanatory tone rather than acute distress. Similar past days (2024-05-08, 2024-05-12) averaged ER_desire ~2-3. Morning timing and functional activity pattern support modest ER_desire. High anxiety trait (GAD-7=12) combined with hormonal context supports worried/sad states. Pain flagged given menstrual context.

Diary cites two concurrent stressors: menstrual cycle (also present in morning EMA, ER=2) and financial concerns (a high-severity recurring stressor — triggered ER=7/receptive on 2024-05-05). Tone is matter-of-fact rather than catastrophizing, suggesting acknowledgment without acute crisis. Behavior is moderately active (outdoor, 91 min walking, moderate screen/keyboard), not showing withdrawal patterns. Most behaviorally similar day (2024-05-08) had ER=1 but diary was blank; content-similar day (2024-05-05) had ER=7. Today's combined stressors and calmer phrasing suggest ER~5. High trait NA (31), PHQ-8 (19), GAD-7 (12) anchor above-baseline negative affect. Pain and worry flagged given menstrual + financial anxiety. PA below baseline; no positive events or social warmth reported.

Diary explicitly states worry about work review during probationary period while daughter is sick — a classic high-stakes work-family conflict. Behavioral data is consistent: 99% stationary morning (home with sick child, not commuting), screen usage dramatically elevated (Z=+20.26 vs personal baseline) suggesting anxious phone-checking, heavy social media (56min) with minimal communication (4min) indicating passive distraction rather than active coping. Yesterday's ER_desire=8 (menstrual+financial stress) places today in an already-elevated distress window. Similar past days with financial/work stress showed ER_desire=7-8, landing today at 5 given morning framing. High Trait NA=31 and PHQ-8=19 amplify the stressor impact. Forecasting=TRUE (explicit concern about future review outcome). NA elevated above personal norm; PA suppressed with no positive signals present.

Afternoon diary confirms the morning stressor persists: sick child at home while trying to maintain work output during a probationary period. Morning ER_desire was 10 (max). Screen usage is extreme (z=+17.92 vs personal baseline), indoor/stationary behavioral profile perfectly matches caregiving-while-working scenario. Similar behavioral days yielded ER_desire 7-8 in 2/3 cases. Profile (PHQ-8=19, GAD-7=12, Trait NA=31) amplifies vulnerability to work-family conflict stressors. No discrepancies across modalities — high NA, worry, and fear states are all active. Receptive to intervention given elevated distress and established pattern of availability.

Third EMA of an escalating crisis day: sick child at home while on work probation (morning), same (afternoon), now adding intense marital conflict (evening). All three diaries signal extreme negative affect with zero positive anchors. Screen use is at Z=+12.27 vs personal baseline — top 1% of all her evenings — consistent with frantic, anxious checking behavior. Near-zero afternoon screen suggests hands-on caregiving. Behavioral evidence fully corroborates the diary's tone of overwhelm, stress, and conflict. With Trait NA=31, PHQ-8=19, and ER_desire already maxed at 10 twice today, the evening state is predicted as severe negative affect, low positive affect, high desire for emotion regulation. Interactions quality is poor (arguing with husband a lot). Forecasting is active (work probation consequences). Person is still completing EMA so available for intervention despite distress.

Morning EMA following three consecutive ER=10 EMAs yesterday (sick child + work probation anxiety + husband argument). Diary reports unexplained fatigue despite adequate sleep — classic pattern for cancer-related fatigue and/or depression-driven exhaustion (PHQ-8=19). No acute crisis mentioned today suggests partial recovery, but emotional residue remains. Behavioral signal strongly validates diary: screen use z=+27.33 above baseline (357 opens, 4h 16m, social-heavy) indicates passive distraction/avoidance; near-sedentary motion (19 min walking) confirms low-energy state. Similar 'tired' diary days show ER range 0-10 depending on co-occurring stressors; absent active crisis today, moderate ER_desire ~4 is calibrated estimate. Menstrual cycle from 05/13 may still contribute to physical fatigue and pain. Consistently reports availability throughout study.

Diary entry 'I got to take a nap in the evening' signals recovery and self-care after extreme 3-day stress peak (ER_desire=10 across three 5/14 entries). The 'got to' phrasing expresses appreciation — a mild positive emotion. Behavioral data fully corroborates: 99% stationary in evening, low mobility (20 min walking), consistent with actual napping. High social app use (1h34m) and heavy evening keyboard activity (2429 chars) suggest social engagement while resting rather than isolation. Profile history confirms napping as an explicit coping mechanism for this person. Relative to her own baseline: PA above average (nap pleasure), NA below average (relief from yesterday's peak), ER desire dropping sharply after crisis. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 anchor the absolute values as moderate-low rather than high positive.

Diary 'I got good sleep last night!' is a strong positive signal. Pattern analysis of 3 prior 'good sleep' morning entries for this person shows ER_desire=0 in all cases. Context: recovery from acute stress peak on May 14 (sick child/arguments). Behavioral data shows normal low-mobility indoor day, slightly elevated walking vs baseline suggesting better energy. High trait NA (31) and PHQ-8=19 prevent NA from dropping to zero. Morning timing before day's stressors emerge. Diary-to-behavior alignment is consistent.

Diary 'I'm not sure!' is ambiguous but historically maps to very low ER_desire (0-1) for this participant. Recent context strongly shapes interpretation: crisis peaked on May 14 (sick child, marital conflict, ER=10), then progressively de-escalated through May 15 (nap relief), with this morning showing clear recovery ('Good sleep!', ER=1). The afternoon 'not sure' reflects post-crisis neutral ambivalence rather than active distress. Behavioral data shows elevated social app use (1h17m) suggesting passive scrolling/distraction, but no distress markers. Screen time is elevated vs hourly baseline but reasonable as daily total. All 4 behaviorally similar days showed ER_desire=0 and non-receptive. Individual-level PA_State marked true because recovery trajectory puts today above this person's typically low PA baseline (trait PA=20); NA_State false as relief from acute crisis places today below their chronically elevated NA baseline (PHQ-8=19, trait NA=31).

Saturday morning EMA. Diary 'I got to sleep in today' is a simple, genuine positive signal — mirrors prior entries like 'Good sleep' (ER=0) and 'I got good sleep last night!' Solo vacation in Dominican Republic, explicitly reporting 'most relaxed and rested in years' — a peak positive exception given high trait NA and depression baseline.
